title: Implement INJURY events in Variant B simulation
description: EventType.INJURY is defined in the enum but never emitted by the simulation engine. Implement injury logic: model a low-probability injury trigger (e.g. from fouls or fatigue), emit an INJURY event, force an emergency substitution if available, and reflect the player's removal from the active roster.

title: Implement CORNER events in Variant B simulation
description: EventType.CORNER is defined in the enum but never emitted by the simulation engine. Implement corner kick logic: detect when a shot goes wide or over near the byline and emit a CORNER event, then model the set-piece delivery and resulting possession/chance.

title: Implement FREE_KICK events in Variant B simulation
description: EventType.FREE_KICK is defined in the enum but never emitted by the simulation engine. Implement free kick logic: when a FOUL is committed, determine whether the resulting set-piece is a direct/indirect free kick, emit FREE_KICK event, and model the delivery outcome (shot, cross, or possession transfer).

title: Implement PENALTY events in Variant B simulation
description: EventType.PENALTY is defined in the enum but never emitted by the simulation engine. Implement penalty kick logic: detect fouls in the penalty area, emit a PENALTY event, and model the penalty outcome (GOAL or SAVE) using shooter vs. keeper attributes.

title: Add game log section to player details page
description: Add a game log section to the player details page showing per-game stats for the player. Display current-season game log plus historical seasons still in storage. Stats sourced from existing season history records — no new persisted aggregates. Group by season, current season first.
acceptance_criteria: - Player details page has a Game Log section showing per-game stats (goals, assists, shots, passes, tackles, cards, minutes played, etc.)

title: Add game log section to player details page
description: Add a game log section to the player details page that shows per-game stats for the player across all available seasons.

title: Save perfect-rated (10.0) match performances to league metadata
description: When a player achieves a 10.0 match rating, persist that performance record in the league metadata (or a dedicated field). The stored record should include the player's full stat line for that match (goals, assists, shots, passes, tackles, rating, etc.) so it can be surfaced in the UI as a hall-of-fame type record.

title: dressBestPlayers: evaluate available formations and pick best player/formation combo
description:
Currently dressBestPlayers() in game.service.ts hardcodes a 4-4-2 shape (1 GK, 4 DEF, 4 MID, 2 FWD) when auto-selecting starters for CPU-controlled teams. It should instead:

1. Enumerate the available (legal) formations for the team.
2. For each formation, score the lineup by summing the overalls of the best-fit players that can fill its slot counts (GK/DEF/MID/FWD distribution).
3. Select the formation + player assignment that yields the highest aggregate rating.
4. Fall back to the current 4-4-2 heuristic if no formations are defined or the team lacks enough players.

Entry point: GameService.dressBestPlayers() (~line 646 in game.service.ts).
Called from: generateLeague, advanceSeason, initializeFirstSeason, and the lineup-refresh path.
close_reason: Implemented: dressBestPlayers now enumerates all formations via FormationLibraryService.getAllFormations(), scores each by summing player overalls, and picks the highest-scoring viable formation. Falls back to the hardcoded 4-4-2 heuristic when no formation can be filled. Updated game.service.spec.ts mocks to include getAllFormations and getDefaultFormationId. All 192 tests pass.

title: Show match-result tooltip on W/D/L form icons in standings
description: Enhance the standings page current-form display so hovering each W/D/L icon reveals a tooltip with the underlying match result detail (opponent and scoreline).
design: Extend the standings form data model to carry per-item context (result type, opponent name/abbr, scoreline, and optionally home/away). Render a tooltip on each form icon in the standings row using existing UI styling conventions. Ensure tooltip positioning and accessibility are robust on desktop and does not break mobile behavior.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Each form icon (W/D/L) in standings shows a tooltip on hover/focus with match result detail. 2) Tooltip content includes at least opponent and scoreline; optional home/away indicator if available. 3) No regression to sorting/layout of standings table. 4) Keyboard focus can access the same tooltip info for accessibility. 5) Add/update tests for form-item mapping and tooltip content rendering.

title: Add single-team schedule view
description: Create a dedicated page/view that shows only one selected team's schedule, including past and upcoming fixtures, match results, and quick navigation to match/watch details.
design: Add a team-focused schedule route and page component that filters league schedule by teamId (home or away). Reuse existing match row styling/patterns where practical. Include season-week grouping or simple chronological list, and provide links to watch/simulated match details. Ensure empty states and missing team handling are covered.
acceptance_criteria: 1) User can open a team schedule view from team context (e.g., Team Details or standings link). 2) View shows only fixtures involving that team, with opponent, home/away indicator, week/date surrogate, score/result if played, and pending status if not played. 3) Entries link to existing watch game or match detail flow where available. 4) Filtering is accurate for both home and away fixtures. 5) Add tests for filtering behavior and key UI states (played/unplayed/empty team schedule).

title: Reduce Variant B tackle event frequency
description: Tackles still appear too frequent relative to interceptions/other turnover outcomes. Current flow often defaults failed passes/carries to TACKLE. Investigate and rebalance turnover event classification so tackle events are less overrepresented while preserving realistic possession turnovers.
design: Audit all TACKLE emission paths in match.simulation.variant-b.service.ts: carry failure path, pass no-target fallback, and determinePassFailureMode default behavior. Introduce calibration-friendly tuning for tackle/interception split (likely in failure-mode selection), and avoid unconditional TACKLED fallback where lane-cut/overhit is more plausible. Keep turnover-winner attribution and existing invariants intact.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Variant B failed-pass and carry turnover logic no longer disproportionately emits TACKLE by default. 2) Add/adjust unit tests to verify balanced tackle vs interception outcomes for representative scenarios. 3) Rating distribution impact remains stable after tackle-frequency changes (no major regressions in focused simulation tests).

title: Test creation
close_reason: Added targeted StatisticsService tests for match aggregates, team season stats, heat map, and passing network; verified with focused test run.
